sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:

<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation

# Check white pixel position

        b = n['boundary']

</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The white pixel moved from (9, 18) to (42, 48) - the blue cross's center! So pressing SPACE cycles to the next shape's center, regardless of where the current shape is. This is much more efficient than I thought!
Now I'm controlling the blue cross at (42, 48). I need to move it to (48, 27) to collect the 4 blue dots.
From (42, 48) to (48, 27):
- DOWN: (48 - 42) / 3 = 2
- LEFT: (27 - 48) / 3 = -7 → 7 LEFT
So 2 DOWN + 7 LEFT = 9 moves.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
